t.hilali-khan: And Thamud, and the people of Lout (Lot), and the dwellers of the wood; such were the confederates
t.itani: And Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Woods—these were the confederates.
t.mubarakpuri: And Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the Dwellers of Al-Aykah; such were the Confederates.
t.qarib: [the tribe of] Thamud, the people of Lot, the woods-dwellers: those were the parties
t.wahiduddin: and the tribe of Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Wood -- these were the confederates
t.shakir: And Samood and the people of Lut and the dwellers of the thicket; these were the parties
t.haleem: Thamud, the people of Lot, and the Forest-Dwellers each formed opposition [against theirs]
t.maariful: and Thamūd and the people of LūT and the people of Aaikah have rejected (the messengers). They were the (strong) groups
t.khattab: Thamûd, the people of Lot, and the residents of the Forest. These were ˹all˺ enemy forces
t.ghali: And Thamud, and the people of Lut (Lot) and the companions of the Thicket; those were the (allied) parties
t.irving: and Thamud, Lots folk, and the inhabitants of the Forest. Those were all partisans
t.iwq: And the tribe of Thamud and the people of Lot and the companions of the thicket. Those are the companies

t_id.kemenag: dan Tsamud, kaum Luth dan penduduk Aikah. Mereka itulah golongan-golongan yang bersekutu (menentang rasul-rasul).
t_id.sabiq: Sebelum mereka, kaum Nûh, bangsa 'Ad, Fir'aun yang memiliki gedung-gedung megah dan kuat bagai gunung, demikian pula Tsamûd, kaum Nabi Lûth dan kaum Nabi Syu'aib, pemilik pepohonan yang rindang, semuanya telah mendustakan para rasul. Mereka bersekongkol melawan rasul-rasul mereka seperti halnya kaummu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Tsamud, kaum Luth dan penduduk Aikah) yakni penduduk kota Al-Ghidhah, mereka adalah kaum Nabi Syuaib a.s. (Mereka itulah golongan-golongan yang bersekutu menentang rasul-rasul).
t_id.iwq: dan Tsamud, kaum Luth dan penduduk Aikah. Mereka itulah golongan-golongan yang bersekutu (menentang rasul-rasul).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وثمود وقوم لوط وأصحاب الأيكة» أي الغيضة، وهم قوم شعيب عليه السلام «أولئك الأحزاب»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَثَمُودُ وَقَوۡمُ لُوطٖ وَأَصۡحَٰبُ لۡـَٔيۡكَةِۚ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ (وَأَصْحَابُ الأَيْكَةِ: أَصْحَابُ الأَشْجَارِ وَالبَسَاتِينِ؛ وَهُمْ قَوْمُ شُعَيْبٍ - عليه السلام)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Tsamud, kaum Luth dan penduduk Aikah) yakni penduduk kota Al-Ghidhah, mereka adalah kaum Nabi Syuaib a.s. (Mereka itulah golongan-golongan yang bersekutu menentang rasul-rasul)

ar: إِن كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ
ar_simple: إِنْ كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ

t.sahih-int: Each of them denied the messengers, so My penalty was justified
t.asad: not one [was there] but gave the lie to the apostles - and thereupon My retribution fell due
t.yusuf-ali: Not one (of them) but rejected the messengers, but My punishment came justly and inevitably (on them)
t.pickthall: Not one of them but did deny the messengers, therefor My doom was justified
t.arberry: not one, that cried not lies to the Messengers, so My retribution was just
t.hilali-khan: Not one of them but belied the Messengers, therefore My Torment was justified
t.itani: None of them but denied the messengers, so My retribution was deserved.
t.mubarakpuri: Not one of them but denied the Messengers; therefore My torment was justified.
t.qarib: each of whom rejected the messengers, so My punishment was justified
t.wahiduddin: There was not one of them but treated their messengers as liars, so My punishment rightly overtook them
t.shakir: There was none of them but called the apostles liars, so just was My retribution
t.haleem: They all rejected the mes-sengers and they were deservedly struck by My punishment
t.maariful: All of them did nothing but declare the messengers as liars; hence, My punishment became due
t.khattab: Each rejected their messenger, so My punishment was justified
t.ghali: Decidedly not one but cried lies to the Messengers, so My punishment came true
t.irving: every last one rejected the messengers, so My punishment was justified
t.iwq: Each of them denied the messengers, so My penalty was justified

t_id.kemenag: Semua mereka itu tidak lain hanyalah mendustakan rasul-rasul, maka pastilah (bagi mereka) azab-Ku.
t_id.sabiq: Masing-masing mereka telah mendustakan rasulnya. Maka mereka berhak menerima siksa-Ku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tidak lain) tiada lain (semuanya) artinya masing-masing dari golongan-golongan yang bersekutu itu (hanyalah mendustakan rasul-rasul) karena mereka telah mendustakan salah seorang dari rasul-rasul itu, ini berarti sama saja dengan mendustakan semua rasul-rasul, karena sesungguhnya seruan dan ajaran mereka satu, yaitu menyeru kepada ajaran tauhid (maka pastilah) wajiblah bagi mereka (azab-Ku).
t_id.iwq: Semua mereka itu tidak lain hanyalah mendustakan rasul-rasul, maka pastilah (bagi mereka) azab-Ku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن» ما «كل» من الأحزاب «إلا كذب الرسل» لأنهم إذا كذبوا واحدا منهم فقد كذبوا جميعهم لأن دعوتهم واحدة، وهي دعوة التوحيد «فحق» وجب «عقاب»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِن كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ (فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ: فَوَجَبَ العِقَابُ عَلَيْهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tidak lain) tiada lain (semuanya) artinya masing-masing dari golongan-golongan yang bersekutu itu (hanyalah mendustakan rasul-rasul) karena mereka telah mendustakan salah seorang dari rasul-rasul itu, ini berarti sama saja dengan mendustakan semua rasul-rasul, karena sesungguhnya seruan dan ajaran mereka satu, yaitu menyeru kepada ajaran tauhid (maka pastilah) wajiblah bagi mereka (azab-Ku)

ar: وَمَا یَنظُرُ هَـٰۤؤُلَاۤءِ إِلَّا صَیۡحَةࣰ وَ ٰحِدَةࣰ مَّا لَهَا مِن فَوَاقࣲ
ar_simple: وَمَا يَنْظُرُ هَٰؤُلَاءِ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً مَا لَهَا مِنْ فَوَاقٍ

t.sahih-int: And these [disbelievers] await not but one blast [of the Horn]; for it there will be no delay
t.asad: And they [who now deny the truth - they, too,] have but to wait for one single blast [of punishment to overtake them]: it shall not be delayed a whit
t.yusuf-ali: These (today) only wait for a single mighty Blast, which (when it comes) will brook no delay
t.pickthall: These wait for but one Shout, there will be no second thereto
t.arberry: These are only awaiting for a single Cry, to which there is no delay
t.hilali-khan: And these only wait for a single Saihah [shout (i.e. the blowing of the Trumpet by the angel Israfil Sarafil)] there will be no pause or ending thereto [till everything will perish except Allah (the only God full of Majesty, Bounty and Honour]
t.itani: These can expect only a single scream, from which there is no recovery.
t.mubarakpuri: And these only wait for a single Sayhah there will be no pause or ending thereto.
t.qarib: And these, too, [who now deny the truth] are only awaiting a single blast, which will not be stopped
t.wahiduddin: they have only to wait for one single blast [of punishment]: it shall not be delayed by one whit
t.shakir: Nor do these await aught but a single cry, there being no delay in it
t.haleem: all the disbelievers here are waiting for is a single blast that cannot be postponed
t.maariful: And these people are waiting for nothing but for a single Cry (the sound of the Trumpet to be blown on Doomsday) that will have no pause
t.khattab: These ˹pagans˺ are awaiting nothing but a single Blast that cannot be stopped
t.ghali: And in no way are these looking for anything except one Shout; in no way is there any recovery from it
t.irving: Those persons only await a single Blast which will [allow for] no delay
t.iwq: And these disbelievers await not but one blast of the Horn; for it there will be no delay

t_id.kemenag: Tidaklah yang mereka tunggu melainkan hanya satu teriakan saja yang tidak ada baginya saat berselang.
t_id.sabiq: Mereka yang mendustakan para rasul itu hanya menunggu satu teriakan keras yang tidak perlu diulang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tiadalah yang ditunggu-tunggu) yang dinantikan (oleh mereka) oleh orang-orang kafir Mekah. (melainkan hanya satu teriakan) yaitu tiupan sangkakala untuk kiamat yang saat itu mereka ditimpa oleh azab (yang tidak ada bagi mereka saat berselang) maksudnya, sesudah itu tidak akan ada saat hidup kembali seperti di dunia. Lafal Fawaaqin dapat pula dibaca Fuwaaqin.
t_id.iwq: Tidaklah yang mereka tunggu melainkan hanya satu teriakan saja yang tidak ada baginya saat berselang.

tafsir.muyassar-ar: وما ينتظر هؤلاء المشركون لحلول العذاب عليهم إن بقوا على شركهم، إلا نفخة واحدة ما لها من رجوع.
tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما ينظر» ينتظر «هؤلاء» أي كفار مكة «إلا صيحة واحدة» هي نفخة القيامة تحل بهم العذاب «ما لها من فواق» بفتح الفاء وضمها: رجوع
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا يَنظُرُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِلَّا صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ مَّا لَهَا مِن فَوَاقٖ (وَمَا يَنظُرُ: مَا يَنْتَظِرُ, صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً: نَفْخَةَ القِيَامَةِ, فَوَاقٍ: رُجُوعٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tiadalah yang ditunggu-tunggu) yang dinantikan (oleh mereka) oleh orang-orang kafir Mekah. (melainkan hanya satu teriakan) yaitu tiupan sangkakala untuk kiamat yang saat itu mereka ditimpa oleh azab (yang tidak ada bagi mereka saat berselang) maksudnya, sesudah itu tidak akan ada saat hidup kembali seperti di dunia. Lafal Fawaaqin dapat pula dibaca Fuwaaqin

ar: وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا عَجِّل لَّنَا قِطَّنَا قَبۡلَ یَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ar_simple: وَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا عَجِّلْ لَنَا قِطَّنَا قَبْلَ يَوْمِ الْحِسَابِ

t.sahih-int: And they say, "Our Lord, hasten for us our share [of the punishment] before the Day of Account
t.asad: As it is, they say [mockingly]: “O our Sustainer! Hasten on to us our share [of punishment even] before the Day of Reckoning!”
t.yusuf-ali: They say: "Our Lord! hasten to us our sentence (even) before the Day of Account
t.pickthall: They say: Our Lord! Hasten on for us our fate before the Day of Reckoning
t.arberry: They say, 'Our Lord, hasten to us our share before the Day of Reckoning
t.hilali-khan: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten to us Qittana (i.e. our Record of good and bad deeds so that we see it) before the Day of Reckoning
t.itani: And they say, “Our Lord, hasten Your writ upon us, before the Day of Account.”
t.mubarakpuri: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten to us Qittana before the Day of Reckoning!"
t.qarib: And they say, "Our Lord, hasten for us our share [of the punishment] before the Day of Account
t.wahiduddin: They say: "Our Lord! Hasten on for us our fate before the Day of Reckoning
t.shakir: And they say: O our Lord! hasten on to us our portion before the day of reckoning
t.haleem: They say, ‘Our Lord! Advance us our share of punishment before the Day of Reckoning!’
t.maariful: And they say, “O our Lord, give us our share (of the punishment) sooner, before the Day of Reckoning.”
t.khattab: They say ˹mockingly˺, “Our Lord! Hasten for us our share ˹of the punishment˺ before the Day of Reckoning.”
t.ghali: And they have said, "Our Lord, hasten (quickly) to us our sentence before the Day of Reckoning
t.irving: They will say: "Our Lord, hasten our doom for us before the Day of Reckoning
t.iwq: And they say, "Our Lord, hasten for us our share of the punishment before the Day of Account

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Ya Tuhan kami cepatkanlah untuk kami azab yang diperuntukkan bagi kami sebelum hari berhisab".
t_id.sabiq: Dengan mencibir, orang-orang kafir mengatakan, "Ya Tuhan kami, segerakanlah siksa kami sebelum datang hari pembalasan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) sewaktu Allah menurunkan firman-Nya, "Adapun orang-orang yang diberikan kepadanya kitabnya dari sebelah kanannya..." (Q.S. Al-Haqqah, 19) ("Ya Rabb kami! Segerakanlah untuk kami catatan amal kami) yakni kitab catatan amal kami (sebelum hari berhisab") mereka mengatakan hal ini dengan nada yang sinis dan mengejek.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Ya Tuhan kami cepatkanlah untuk kami azab yang diperuntukkan bagi kami sebelum hari berhisab".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا» لما نزل (فأما من أوتي كتابه بيمينه) الخ «ربنا عجل لنا قطنا» أي كتاب أعمالنا «قبل يوم الحساب» قالوا ذلك استهزاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَا عَجِّل لَّنَا قِطَّنَا قَبۡلَ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ (قِطَّنَا: نَصِيبَنَا مِنَ العَذَابِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) sewaktu Allah menurunkan firman-Nya, "Adapun orang-orang yang diberikan kepadanya kitabnya dari sebelah kanannya..." (Q.S. Al-Haqqah, 19) ("Ya Rabb kami! Segerakanlah untuk kami catatan amal kami) yakni kitab catatan amal kami (sebelum hari berhisab") mereka mengatakan hal ini dengan nada yang sinis dan mengejek

ar: ٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا یَقُولُونَ وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلۡأَیۡدِۖ إِنَّهُۥۤ أَوَّابٌ
ar_simple: اصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَاذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا دَاوُودَ ذَا الْأَيْدِ ۖ إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ

t.sahih-int: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Allah]
t.asad: [But] bear thou with patience whatever they may say, and remember Our servant David, him who was endowed with [so much] inner strength! He, verily, would always turn unto Us
t.yusuf-ali: Have patience at what they say, and remember our servant David, the man of strength: for he ever turned (to Allah)
t.pickthall: Bear with what they say, and remember Our bondman David, lord of might, Lo! he was ever turning in repentance (toward Allah)
t.arberry: Bear patiently what they say, and remember Our servant David, the man of might; he was a penitent
t.hilali-khan: Be patient (O Muhammad SAW) of what they say, and remember Our slave Dawud (David), endued with power. Verily, he was ever oft-returning in all matters and in repentance (toward Allah)
t.itani: Be patient in the face of what they say, and mention Our servant David, the resourceful. He was obedient.
t.mubarakpuri: Be patient of what they say and remember Our slave Dawud, endued with Al-Ayd. Verily, he was ever oft-returning in all matters and in repentance.
t.qarib: Be patient over what they say, and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Us]
t.wahiduddin: Bear with their words patiently. Remember Our servant David, a man of strength who always turned to Us
t.shakir: Bear patiently what they say, and remember Our servant Dawood, the possessor of power; surely he was frequent in returning (to Allah)
t.haleem: Bear their words patiently [Prophet]. Remember Our servant David, a man of strength who always turned to Us
t.maariful: Bear patiently with what they say, and remember Our servant Dawūd, the man of might. Surely he was ever-turning to Allah
t.khattab: Be patient ˹O Prophet˺ with what they say. And remember Our servant, David, the man of strength. Indeed, he ˹constantly˺ turned ˹to Allah˺
t.ghali: (Endure) patiently what they say; and remember Our bondman Dawud (David) (a man) possessing prowess (Literally: hands). Surely he was a constant resorter (to Allah)
t.irving: Endure what they are saying and remember how Our servant David commanded assistance; he was attentive [to God]
t.iwq: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back to Allah

t_id.kemenag: Bersabarlah atas segala apa yang mereka katakan; dan ingatlah hamba Kami Daud yang mempunyai kekuatan; sesungguhnya dia amat taat (kepada Tuhan).
t_id.sabiq: Bersabarlah, hai Muhammad, atas apa yang dikatakan oleh orang-orang musyrik kepadamu. Ingatlah hamba Kami, Dâwûd, yang memiliki kekuatan agama dan dunia. Sesungguhnya dia selalu kembali kepada Allah dalam setiap keadaan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Allah swt. berfirman, ("Bersabarlah atas segala apa yang mereka katakan; ingatlah hamba Kami Daud yang mempunyai kekuatan) dalam beribadah; tersebutlah bahwa dia sepanjang tahun selalu berpuasa sehari dan berbuka sehari; bangun pada tengah malam untuk melakukan salat, kemudian tidur selama sepertiga malam dan seperenam malam harinya lagi ia gunakan untuk salat (sesungguhnya dia amat taat) yakni selalu mengerjakan hal-hal yang menjadi keridaan Allah swt.
t_id.iwq: Bersabarlah atas segala apa yang mereka katakan; dan ingatlah hamba Kami Daud yang mempunyai kekuatan; sesungguhnya dia amat taat (kepada Tuhan).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: قال تعالى: «اصبر على ما يقولون واذكر عبدنا داود ذا الأيد» أي القوة في العبادة كان يصوم يوما ويفطر يوما ويقوم نصف الليل وينام ثلثه ويقوم سدسه «إنه أوَّاب» رجّاع إلى مرضاة الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: ٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلۡأَيۡدِۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ (ذَا الْأَيْدِ: صَاحِبَ القَوَّةِ عَلَى الطَّاعَةِ، وَفِي الحَرْبِ, أَوَّابٌ: كَثِيرُ الرُّجُوعِ إِلَى مَا يُرْضِي اللهَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Allah swt. berfirman, ("Bersabarlah atas segala apa yang mereka katakan; ingatlah hamba Kami Daud yang mempunyai kekuatan) dalam beribadah; tersebutlah bahwa dia sepanjang tahun selalu berpuasa sehari dan berbuka sehari; bangun pada tengah malam untuk melakukan salat, kemudian tidur selama sepertiga malam dan seperenam malam harinya lagi ia gunakan untuk salat (sesungguhnya dia amat taat) yakni selalu mengerjakan hal-hal yang menjadi keridaan Allah swt

ar: إِنَّا سَخَّرۡنَا ٱلۡجِبَالَ مَعَهُۥ یُسَبِّحۡنَ بِٱلۡعَشِیِّ وَٱلۡإِشۡرَاقِ
ar_simple: إِنَّا سَخَّرْنَا الْجِبَالَ مَعَهُ يُسَبِّحْنَ بِالْعَشِيِّ وَالْإِشْرَاقِ

t.sahih-int: Indeed, We subjected the mountains [to praise] with him, exalting [Allah] in the [late] afternoon and [after] sunrise
t.asad: [and for this,] behold, We caused the mountains to join him in extolling Our limitless glory at eventide and at sunrise
t.yusuf-ali: It was We that made the hills declare, in unison with him, Our Praises, at eventide and at break of day
t.pickthall: Lo! We subdued the hills to hymn the praises (of their Lord) with him at nightfall and sunrise
t.arberry: With him We subjected the mountains to give glory at evening and sunrise
t.hilali-khan: Verily, We made the mountains to glorify Our Praises with him [Dawud (David)] in the 'Ashi (i.e. after the mid-day till sunset) and Ishraq (i.e. after the sunrise till mid-day)
t.itani: We committed the mountains to glorify with him, in the evening and at daybreak.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, We made the mountains to glorify Our praises with him in the `Ashi and Ishraq.
t.qarib: We subjected the mountains to join him in praising Our limitless glory in the evening and daybreak
t.wahiduddin: We made the mountains join him in glorifying Us at sunset and sunrise
t.shakir: Surely We made the mountains to sing the glory (of Allah) in unison with him at the evening and the sunrise
t.haleem: We made the mountains join him in glorifying Us at sunset and sunrise
t.maariful: We had subjugated the mountains to join him (in) making tasbīH (i.e. pronouncing Allah’s purity) at evening and sunrise
t.khattab: We truly subjected the mountains to hymn ˹Our praises˺ along with him in the evening and after sunrise
t.ghali: Surely to him We subjected the mountains with him to extol at nightfall and sunshine
t.irving: We forced the mountains to glorify [God] with him at evening and sunrise
t.iwq: Indeed, We subjected the mountains to praise with him, exalting Allah in the late afternoon and after sunrise

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami menundukkan gunung-gunung untuk bertasbih bersama dia (Daud) di waktu petang dan pagi,
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Kami menundukkan gunung-gunung agar manfaat yang terkandung di dalamnya dapat dieksploitasi oleh Dâwûd, dan agar gunung-gunung itu bertasbih bersama Dâwûd menyucikan Allah dari segala bentuk kekurangan pada setiap pagi dan petang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami menundukkan gunung-gunung untuk bertasbih bersama dia di waktu petang) di waktu salat Isyak (dan pagi) di waktu salat Duha, yaitu di waktu matahari mencapai sepenggalah.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami menundukkan gunung-gunung untuk bertasbih bersama dia (Daud) di waktu petang dan pagi,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا سخرنا الجبال معه يسبحن» بتسبيحه «بالعشيّ» وقت صلاة العشاء «والإشراق» وقت صلاة الضحى وهو أن تشرق الشمس ويتناهى ضوءُها
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا سَخَّرۡنَا ٱلۡجِبَالَ مَعَهُۥ يُسَبِّحۡنَ بِٱلۡعَشِيِّ وَٱلۡإِشۡرَاقِ (بِالْعَشِيِّ: آخِرِ النَّهَارِ, وَالْإِشْرَاقِ: أَوَّلِ النَّهَارِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami menundukkan gunung-gunung untuk bertasbih bersama dia di waktu petang) di waktu salat Isyak (dan pagi) di waktu salat Duha, yaitu di waktu matahari mencapai sepenggalah

ar: وَٱلطَّیۡرَ مَحۡشُورَةࣰۖ كُلࣱّ لَّهُۥۤ أَوَّابࣱ
ar_simple: وَالطَّيْرَ مَحْشُورَةً ۖ كُلٌّ لَهُ أَوَّابٌ

t.sahih-int: And the birds were assembled, all with him repeating [praises]
t.asad: and [likewise] the birds in their assemblies: [together] they all Would turn again and again unto Him [who had created them]
t.yusuf-ali: And the birds gathered (in assemblies): all with him did turn (to Allah)
t.pickthall: And the birds assembled; all were turning unto Him
t.arberry: and the birds, duly mustered, every one to him reverting
t.hilali-khan: And (so did) the birds assembled: all with him [Dawud (David)] did turn (to Allah i.e. glorified His Praises)
t.itani: And the birds, gathered together. All obedient to him.
t.mubarakpuri: And (so did) the birds assembled, all obedient to him.
t.qarib: and the birds to assemble, all repeating [praises] with him
t.wahiduddin: and the birds, too, in flocks, all turned to Him
t.shakir: And the birds gathered together; all joined in singing with him
t.haleem: and the birds, too, in flocks, all echoed his praise
t.maariful: and the birds as well, mustered together. All were turning to Allah with him
t.khattab: And ˹We subjected˺ the birds, flocking together. All turned to him ˹echoing his hymns˺
t.ghali: And the birds, mustered all to him a constant resorter
t.irving: and the birds were summoned and each was [made] attentive to him
t.iwq: And the birds were assembled, all with him repeating praises

t_id.kemenag: dan (Kami tundukkan pula) burung-burung dalam keadaan terkumpul. Masing-masingnya amat taat kepada Allah.
t_id.sabiq: Kami pun menundukkan burung-burung yang terkumpul dari berbagai jenis dan tempat untuknya. Seluruh burung dan gunung itu tunduk pada kemauan Dâwûd. Dia dapat memanfaatkannya dengan cara yang dia kehendaki untuk kepentingan umum.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan) Kami tundukkan pula (burung-burung dalam keadaan berkumpul) berkumpul untuk bertasbih bersama dengan dia. (Masing-masing) dari gunung-gunung dan burung-burung itu (amat taat kepada-Nya) taat bertasbih kepada-Nya.
t_id.iwq: dan (Kami tundukkan pula) burung-burung dalam keadaan terkumpul. Masing-masingnya amat taat kepada Allah.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «و» سخرنا «الطير محشورة» مجموعة إليه تسبح معه «كل» من الجبال والطير «له أوَّاب» رجّاع إلى طاعته بالتسبيح
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱلطَّيۡرَ مَحۡشُورَةٗۖ كُلّٞ لَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٞ (مَحْشُورَةً: مَجْمُوعَةً, أَوَّابٌ: مُطِيعٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan) Kami tundukkan pula (burung-burung dalam keadaan berkumpul) berkumpul untuk bertasbih bersama dengan dia. (Masing-masing) dari gunung-gunung dan burung-burung itu (amat taat kepada-Nya) taat bertasbih kepada-Nya

ar: وَشَدَدۡنَا مُلۡكَهُۥ وَءَاتَیۡنَـٰهُ ٱلۡحِكۡمَةَ وَفَصۡلَ ٱلۡخِطَابِ
ar_simple: وَشَدَدْنَا مُلْكَهُ وَآتَيْنَاهُ الْحِكْمَةَ وَفَصْلَ الْخِطَابِ

t.sahih-int: And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and discernment in speech
t.asad: And We strengthened his dominion, and bestowed upon him wisdom and sagacity in judgment
t.yusuf-ali: We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment in speech and decision
t.pickthall: We made his kingdom strong and gave him wisdom and decisive speech
t.arberry: We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and speech decisive
t.hilali-khan: We made his kingdom strong and gave him Al-Hikmah (Prophethood, etc.) and sound judgement in speech and decision
t.itani: And We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and decisive speech.
t.mubarakpuri: We made his kingdom strong and gave him Al-Hikmah and sound judgement in speech and decision.
t.qarib: And We strengthened his kingdom, and gave him wisdom and keen judgment
t.wahiduddin: We made his kingdom strong, and bestowed upon him wisdom and sagacity in judgement
t.shakir: And We strengthened his kingdom and We gave him wisdom and a clear judgment
t.haleem: We strengthened his kingdom; We gave him wisdom and a decisive way of speaking
t.maariful: And We made his kingdom strong, and gave him wisdom and decisive speech
t.khattab: We strengthened his kingship, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment
t.ghali: And We upheld his kingdom and brought him (the) Wisdom and decisive speech (Literally: the verdict, the address)
t.irving: We strengthened his control and gave him wisdom and distinction as an orator
t.iwq: And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and discernment in speech

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami kuatkan kerajaannya dan Kami berikan kepadanya hikmahdan kebijaksanaan dalam menyelesaikan perselisihan.
t_id.sabiq: Kami pun menguatkan kerajaannya. Selain itu, ia Kami berikan kenabian dan kemampuan membedakan antara yang benar dan batil.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami kuatkan kerajaannya) Kami kuatkan kerajaannya itu dengan para penjaga dan bala tentara; dan setiap malam mihrab Nabi Daud selalu dijaga oleh tiga puluh ribu pasukan (dan Kami berikan, kepadanya hikmah) yakni, kenabian dan ketepatan dalam berbagai perkara (dan kebijaksanaan dalam menyelesaikan perselisihan") yaitu penjelasan yang memuaskan dalam semua urusan.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami kuatkan kerajaannya dan Kami berikan kepadanya hikmahdan kebijaksanaan dalam menyelesaikan perselisihan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وشددنا ملكه» قوَّيناه بالجرس والجنود وكان يحرس محرابه في كل ليلة ثلاثون ألف رجل «وآتيناه الحكمة» النبوة والإصابة في الأمور «وفصل الخطاب» البيان الشافي في كل قصد
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَشَدَدۡنَا مُلۡكَهُۥ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ ٱلۡحِكۡمَةَ وَفَصۡلَ ٱلۡخِطَابِ (وَشَدَدْنَا مُلْكَهُ: قَوَّيْنَا مُلْكَهُ بِالهَيْبَةِ، وَالتَّمْكِينِ، وَالنَّصْرِ, الْحِكْمَةَ: النُّبَّوةَ, وَفَصْلَ الْخِطَابِ: البَيَانَ الشَّافِيَ وَالفَصْلَ فِي الكَلَامِ وَالحُكْمِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami kuatkan kerajaannya) Kami kuatkan kerajaannya itu dengan para penjaga dan bala tentara; dan setiap malam mihrab Nabi Daud selalu dijaga oleh tiga puluh ribu pasukan (dan Kami berikan, kepadanya hikmah) yakni, kenabian dan ketepatan dalam berbagai perkara (dan kebijaksanaan dalam menyelesaikan perselisihan") yaitu penjelasan yang memuaskan dalam semua urusan

ar: ۞ وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ نَبَؤُا۟ ٱلۡخَصۡمِ إِذۡ تَسَوَّرُوا۟ ٱلۡمِحۡرَابَ
ar_simple: ۞ وَهَلْ أَتَاكَ نَبَأُ الْخَصْمِ إِذْ تَسَوَّرُوا الْمِحْرَابَ

t.sahih-int: And has there come to you the news of the adversaries, when they climbed over the wall of [his] prayer chamber
t.asad: AND YET, has the story of the litigants come within thy ken - [the story of the two] who surmounted the walls of the sanctuary [in which David prayed]
t.yusuf-ali: Has the Story of the Disputants reached thee? Behold, they climbed over the wall of the private chamber
t.pickthall: And hath the story of the litigants come unto thee? How they climbed the wall into the royal chamber
t.arberry: Has the tiding of the dispute come to thee? When they scaled the Sanctuary
t.hilali-khan: And has the news of the litigants reached you? When they climbed over the wall into (his) Mihrab (a praying place or a private room)
t.itani: Has the story of the two disputants reached you? When they scaled the sanctuary?
t.mubarakpuri: And has the news of the litigants reached you When they climbed over the wall into (his) Mihrab (private chamber of worship);
t.qarib: And has the story come to you of the disputants who climbed over the wall of [his] prayer chamber
t.wahiduddin: Have you heard the story of the disputants who entered his chamber by climbing over the wall
t.shakir: And has there come to you the story of the litigants, when they made an entry into the private chamber by ascending over the walls
t.haleem: Have you heard the story of the two litigants who climbed into his private quarters
t.maariful: Has there come to you the story of the litigants, when they entered the sanctuary by climbing over the wall
t.khattab: Has the story of the two plaintiffs, who scaled the ˹wall of David’s˺ sanctuary, reached you ˹O Prophet˺
t.ghali: And has the tiding of the adversaries come up to you? As they scaled the chamber
t.irving: Has news of the litigants ever reached you, when they climbed over the wall into the sanctuary
t.iwq: And has there come to you the news of the adversaries, when they climbed over the wall of his prayer chamber

t_id.kemenag: Dan adakah sampai kepadamu berita orang-orang yang berperkara ketika mereka memanjat pagar?
t_id.sabiq: Apakah telah sampai kepadamu, hai Muhammad, berita tentang orang yang berperkara dan datang kepada Dâwûd melalui pagar mihrab tempat beribadah, bukan dari pintunya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan adakah) Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung makna Ta'ajjub dan Tasywiq, atau dengan kata lain mengandung makna yang mendorong dan merangsang pendengar untuk mendengarkan kalimat-kalimat selanjutnya (sampai kepadamu) hai Muhammad (berita orang-orang yang berperkara ketika mereka memanjat pagar mihrab?) yaitu mihrab Nabi Daud, yang dimaksud adalah mesjidnya; demikian itu terjadi karena mereka dilarang masuk, sebab Nabi Daud sedang beribadah. Akhirnya mereka masuk dengan memanjat pagar mihrabnya. Makna ayat ialah apakah kamu telah mendengar berita dan kisah mereka?
t_id.iwq: Dan adakah sampai kepadamu berita orang-orang yang berperkara ketika mereka memanjat pagar?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهل» معنى الاستفهام هنا التعجيب والتشويق إلى استماع ما بعده «أتاك» يا محمد «نبأ الخصم إذ تسوَّروا المحراب» محراب داود: أي مسجده حيث منعوا الدخول عليه من الباب لشغله بالعبادة، أي خبرهم وقصتهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ نَبَؤُاْ ٱلۡخَصۡمِ إِذۡ تَسَوَّرُواْ ٱلۡمِحۡرَابَ (نَبَأُ: خَبَرُ, الْخَصْمِ: المُتَخَاصِمِينَ, الْمِحْرَابَ: مَكَانَ عِبَادَتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan adakah) Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung makna Ta'ajjub dan Tasywiq, atau dengan kata lain mengandung makna yang mendorong dan merangsang pendengar untuk mendengarkan kalimat-kalimat selanjutnya (sampai kepadamu) hai Muhammad (berita orang-orang yang berperkara ketika mereka memanjat pagar mihrab?) yaitu mihrab Nabi Daud, yang dimaksud adalah mesjidnya; demikian itu terjadi karena mereka dilarang masuk, sebab Nabi Daud sedang beribadah. Akhirnya mereka masuk dengan memanjat pagar mihrabnya. Makna ayat ialah apakah kamu telah mendengar berita dan kisah mereka

ar: إِذۡ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ دَاوُۥدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنۡهُمۡۖ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفۡۖ خَصۡمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعۡضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعۡضࣲ فَٱحۡكُم بَیۡنَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تُشۡطِطۡ وَٱهۡدِنَاۤ إِلَىٰ سَوَاۤءِ ٱلصِّرَ ٰطِ
ar_simple: إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلَىٰ دَاوُودَ فَفَزِعَ مِنْهُمْ ۖ قَالُوا لَا تَخَفْ ۖ خَصْمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعْضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تُشْطِطْ وَاهْدِنَا إِلَىٰ سَوَاءِ الصِّرَاطِ

t.sahih-int: When they entered upon David and he was alarmed by them? They said, "Fear not. [We are] two adversaries, one of whom has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth and do not exceed [it] and guide us to the sound path
t.asad: As they came upon David, and he shrank back in fear from them, they said: “Fear not! [We are but] two litigants. One of us has wronged the other: so judge thou between us with justice, and deviate not from what is right, and show [both of] us the way to rectitude
t.yusuf-ali: When they entered the presence of David, and he was terrified of them, they said: "Fear not: we are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other: Decide now between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, but guide us to the even Path
t.pickthall: How they burst in upon David, and he was afraid of them. They said: Be not afraid! (We are) two litigants, one of whom hath wronged the other, therefor judge aright between us; be not unjust; and show us the fair way
t.arberry: when they entered upon David, and he took fright at them; and they said, 'Fear not; two disputants we are -- one of us has injured the other; so judge between us justly, and transgress not, and guide us to the right path
t.hilali-khan: When they entered in upon Dawud (David), he was terrified of them, they said: "Fear not! (We are) two litigants, one of whom has wronged the other, therefore judge between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, and guide us to the Right Way
t.itani: When they entered upon David, and he was startled by them. They said, “Do not fear. Two disputants; one of us has wronged the other; so judge between us fairly, and do not be biased, and guide us to the straight way.”
t.mubarakpuri: When they entered in upon Dawud, he was terrified of them. They said: "Fear not! (We are) two litigants, one of us has wronged the other, therefore judge between us with truth, and treat us not with injustice, and guide us to the right way.
t.qarib: When they entered upon David, he was alarmed, [but] they said, "Do not be afraid. [We are] two disputants. One of us has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth, and do not be unjust, and guide us to the right way
t.wahiduddin: When they reached David, he took fright, but they said, "Do not be afraid. We are two disputants, one of whom has wronged the other: judge between us fairly, do not be unjust, and guide us to the right path
t.shakir: When they entered in upon Dawood and he was frightened at them, they said: Fear not; two litigants, of whom one has acted wrongfully towards the other, therefore decide between us with justice, and do not act unjustly, and guide us to the right way
t.haleem: When they reached David, he took fright, but they said, ‘Do not be afraid. We are two litigants, one of whom has wronged the other: judge between us fairly- do not be unjust- and guide us to the right path
t.maariful: When they entered upon Dawūd, and he was scared of them, they said, “Be not scared. We are two litigants; one of us has wronged the other, so decide between us with truth, and do not cross the limits, and guide us to the right path
t.khattab: When they came into David’s presence, he was startled by them. They said, “Have no fear. ˹We are merely˺ two in a dispute: one of us has wronged the other. So judge between us with truth—do not go beyond ˹it˺—and guide us to the right way
t.ghali: As they entered upon Dawud; then he was alarmed of them. They said, "Do not fear (anything); two adversaries we are. One of us has been inequitable to the other, (Literally: some of us against some "others") so judge between us with the truth, and do not act unjudiciously, and guide us to the level path
t.irving: Thus they burst in upon David so he was startled by them. They said: "Don´t be afraid: [we are] two litigants, one of whom has injured the other; so judge correctly between us and do not act too stern. Guide us along the Level Road
t.iwq: When they entered upon David and he was alarmed by them? They said, "Fear not. We are two adversaries, one of whom has wronged the other, so judge between us with truth and do not exceed it and guide us to the sound path

t_id.kemenag: Ketika mereka masuk (menemui) Daud lalu ia terkejut karena kedatangan) mereka. Mereka berkata: "Janganlah kamu merasa takut; (kami) adalah dua orang yang berperkara yang salah seorang dari kami berbuat zalim kepada yang lain; maka berilah keputusan antara kami dengan adil dan janganlah kamu menyimpang dari kebenaran dan tunjukilah kami ke jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Yaitu ketika mereka masuk menemui Dâwûd, lalu ia pun merasa takut dan khawatir. Mereka berkata, "Janganlah kamu merasa takut, kami adalah dua orang yang sedang berperkara. Kami saling merugikan satu sama lain. Maka berilah kami keputusan yang adil dan janganlah berlaku zalim. Tunjukilah kami kepada jalan yang benar."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ketika mereka masuk menemui Daud lalu ia terkejut karena kedatangan mereka. Mereka berkata, "Janganlah kamu merasa takut) kami (adalah dua orang yang bersengketa) menurut suatu pendapat dikatakan, bahwa yang bersengketa itu adalah dua golongan, demikian itu supaya sesuai dengan dhamir jamak yang sebelumnya. Menurut pendapat yang lain disebutkan bahwa orang yang bersengketa itu dua orang, sedangkan dhamir jamak diartikan dengannya. Lafal Al-Khashmu dapat diartikan untuk satu orang atau lebih. Kedua orang itu adalah dua malaikat yang menjelma menjadi dua orang yang sedang bersengketa. Persengketaan yang terjadi di antara keduanya hanyalah sebagai perumpamaan, dimaksud untuk mengingatkan Nabi Daud a.s. atas apa yang telah dilakukannya. Karena ia mempunyai istri sebanyak sembilan puluh sembilan orang wanita. Tetapi sekalipun demikian ia melamar istri orang lain yang hanya mempunyai seorang istri kemudian ia mengawininya dan menggaulinya (salah seorang dari kami berbuat zalim kepada yang lain, maka berilah keputusan antara kami dengan adil dan janganlah kamu menyimpang dari kebenaran) janganlah kamu berlaku berat sebelah (dan tunjukilah kami) bimbinglah kami (ke jalan yang lurus) yakni keputusan yang pertengahan dan adil.
t_id.iwq: Ketika mereka masuk (menemui) Daud lalu ia terkejut karena kedatangan) mereka. Mereka berkata: "Janganlah kamu merasa takut; (kami) adalah doa orang yang berperkara yang salat seorang dari kami berbuat zalim kepada yang lain; maka berilah keputusan antara kami dengan adil dan janganlah kamu menyimpang dari kebenaran dan tunjukilah kami ke jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إذ دخلوا على داود ففزع منهم قالوا لا تخف» نحن «خصمان» قيل فريقان ليطابق ما قبله من ضمير الجمع، وقيل اثنان والضمير بمعناهما، والخصم يطلق على الواحد وأكثر، وهما ملكان جاءا في صورة خصمين وقع لهما ما ذكر على سبيل الغرض لتنبيه داود عليه السلام على ما وقع منه وكان له تسع وتسعون امرأة وطلب امرأة شخص ليس له غيرها وتزوجها ودخل بها «بغى بعضنا على بعض فاحكم بيننا بالحق ولا تشطط» تَجُرْ «واهدنا» أرشدنا «إلى سواء الصراط» وسط الطريق الصواب
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِذۡ دَخَلُواْ عَلَىٰ دَاوُۥدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنۡهُمۡۖ قَالُواْ لَا تَخَفۡۖ خَصۡمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعۡضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تُشۡطِطۡ وَٱهۡدِنَآ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ (فَفَزِعَ: ارْتَاعَ, وَلَا تُشْطِطْ: لَا تَجُرْ فِي حُكْمِكَ، وَلَا تَظْلِمْ, سَوَاءَ الصِّرَاطِ: وَسَطِ الطَّرِيقِ الصَّوَابِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ketika mereka masuk menemui Daud lalu ia terkejut karena kedatangan mereka. Mereka berkata, "Janganlah kamu merasa takut) kami (adalah dua orang yang bersengketa) menurut suatu pendapat dikatakan, bahwa yang bersengketa itu adalah dua golongan, demikian itu supaya sesuai dengan dhamir jamak yang sebelumnya. Menurut pendapat yang lain disebutkan bahwa orang yang bersengketa itu dua orang, sedangkan dhamir jamak diartikan dengannya. Lafal Al-Khashmu dapat diartikan untuk satu orang atau lebih. Kedua orang itu adalah dua malaikat yang menjelma menjadi dua orang yang sedang bersengketa. Persengketaan yang terjadi di antara keduanya hanyalah sebagai perumpamaan, dimaksud untuk mengingatkan Nabi Daud a.s. atas apa yang telah dilakukannya. Karena ia mempunyai istri sebanyak sembilan puluh sembilan orang wanita. Tetapi sekalipun demikian ia melamar istri orang lain yang hanya mempunyai seorang istri kemudian ia mengawininya dan menggaulinya (salah seorang dari kami berbuat zalim kepada yang lain, maka berilah keputusan antara kami dengan adil dan janganlah kamu menyimpang dari kebenaran) janganlah kamu berlaku berat sebelah (dan tunjukilah kami) bimbinglah kami (ke jalan yang lurus) yakni keputusan yang pertengahan dan adil

ar: إِنَّ هَـٰذَاۤ أَخِی لَهُۥ تِسۡعࣱ وَتِسۡعُونَ نَعۡجَةࣰ وَلِیَ نَعۡجَةࣱ وَ ٰحِدَةࣱ فَقَالَ أَكۡفِلۡنِیهَا وَعَزَّنِی فِی ٱلۡخِطَابِ
ar_simple: إِنَّ هَٰذَا أَخِي لَهُ تِسْعٌ وَتِسْعُونَ نَعْجَةً وَلِيَ نَعْجَةٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَقَالَ أَكْفِلْنِيهَا وَعَزَّنِي فِي الْخِطَابِ

t.sahih-int: Indeed this, my brother, has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe; so he said, 'Entrust her to me,' and he overpowered me in speech
t.asad: “Behold, this is my brother: he has ninety-nine ewes, whereas I have [only] one ewe - and yet he said, ‘Make her over to me,’ and forcibly prevailed against me in this [our] dispute.”
t.yusuf-ali: This man is my brother: He has nine and ninety ewes, and I have (but) one: Yet he says, 'commit her to my care,' and is (moreover) harsh to me in speech
t.pickthall: Lo! this my brother hath ninety and nine ewes while I had one ewe; and he said: Entrust it to me, and he conquered me in speech
t.arberry: Behold, this my brother has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe. So he said, "Give her into my charge"; and he overcame me in the argument
t.hilali-khan: Verily, this my brother (in religion) has ninety nine ewes, while I have (only) one ewe, and he says: "Hand it over to me, and he overpowered me in speech
t.itani: “This brother of mine has ninety nine ewes, and I have one ewe, and he said, ‘Entrust it to me,’ and he pressured me with words.”
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, this my brother (in religion) has ninety-nine ewes, while I have (only) one ewe, and he says: "Hand it over to me, and he overpowered me in speech."
t.qarib: This is my brother. He has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe, so he said, 'Entrust it to me,' and got the better of me with his words
t.wahiduddin: This brother of mine has ninety-nine ewes and I have only one. He said, "Let me have charge of it," and got the better of me with his words
t.shakir: Surely this is my brother; he has ninety-nine ewes and I have a single ewe; but he said: Make it over to me, and he has prevailed against me in discourse
t.haleem: This is my brother. He had ninety-nine ewes and I just the one, and he said, “Let me take charge of her,” and overpowered me with his words.’
t.maariful: This is my brother; he has ninety nine ewes, and I have a single ewe; still he said, ‘Give it into my charge.’ And he overpowered me in speech.”
t.khattab: This is my brother. He has ninety-nine sheep while I have ˹only˺ one. ˹Still˺ he asked me to give it up to him, overwhelming me with ˹his˺ argument.”
t.ghali: Surely this my brother has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe. So he said to me, ‘Let me be her sponsor’ and he got the better of me in speech." (Literally: was mightier in the address)
t.irving: This is my brother; he has ninety-nine ewes while I have but a single ewe. He has said: "Turn her over to me," and has spoken harshly to me
t.iwq: Indeed this, my brother, has ninety-nine ewes, and I have one ewe; so he said, 'Entrust her to me,' and he overpowered me in speech

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya saudaraku ini mempunyai sembilan puluh sembilan ekor kambing betina dan aku mempunyai seekor saja. Maka dia berkata: "Serahkanlah kambingmu itu kepadaku dan dia mengalahkan aku dalam perdebatan".
t_id.sabiq: Salah seorang dari dua yang berperkara itu berkata, "Saudaraku ini mempunyai sembilan puluh sembilan ekor kambing betina dan saya mempunya seekor saja. Ia lalu berkata kepadaku, 'Biarkan aku yang memeliharanya bersama semua kambing-kambingku. ' Ia pun menang dalam perdebatan itu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya saudaraku ini) maksudnya, saudara seagamaku ini (mempunyai sembilan puluh sembilan ekor kambing) ini sebagai kata kiasan dari istri (dan aku mempunyai seekor saja. Maka dia berkata, 'Serahkanlah kambing itu kepadaku) yakni jadikanlah aku sebagai suaminya (dan dia mengalahkan aku) atau dia menang atas diriku (dalam perdebatan'") yakni dalam sengketa ini, dan lawannya pun mengalah.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya saudaraku ini mempunyai sembilan puluh sembilan ekor kambing betina dan aku mempunyai seekor saja. Maka dia berkata: "Serahkanlah kambingmu itu kepadaku dan dia mengalahkan aku dalam perdebatan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن هذا أخي» أي على ديني «له تسع وتسعون نعجة» يعبر بها عن المرأة «وليَ نعجة واحدة فقال أكفلنيها» أي اجعلني كافلها «وعزني» غلبني «في الخطاب» أي الدال، وأقره الآخر على ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ هَٰذَآ أَخِي لَهُۥ تِسۡعٞ وَتِسۡعُونَ نَعۡجَةٗ وَلِيَ نَعۡجَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ فَقَالَ أَكۡفِلۡنِيهَا وَعَزَّنِي فِي ٱلۡخِطَابِ (أَكْفِلْنِيهَا: أَعْطِنِيهَا، وَانْزِلْ لِي عَنْهَا, وَعَزَّنِي فِي الْخِطَابِ: غَلَبَنِي فيِ الكَلَامِ، وَاشْتَدَّ عَلَيَّ فِيهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya saudaraku ini) maksudnya, saudara seagamaku ini (mempunyai sembilan puluh sembilan ekor kambing) ini sebagai kata kiasan dari istri (dan aku mempunyai seekor saja. Maka dia berkata, 'Serahkanlah kambing itu kepadaku) yakni jadikanlah aku sebagai suaminya (dan dia mengalahkan aku) atau dia menang atas diriku (dalam perdebatan'") yakni dalam sengketa ini, dan lawannya pun mengalah

ar: قَالَ لَقَدۡ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعۡجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِیرࣰا مِّنَ ٱلۡخُلَطَاۤءِ لَیَبۡغِی بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٍ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَقَلِیلࣱ مَّا هُمۡۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُۥدُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّـٰهُ فَٱسۡتَغۡفَرَ رَبَّهُۥ وَ̅خَ̅رَّ̅ ̅رَ̅ا̅كِ̅عࣰ̅ا̅ وَأَنَابَ ۩
ar_simple: قَالَ لَقَدْ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعْجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِ ۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِنَ الْخُلَطَاءِ لَيَبْغِي بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَقَلِيلٌ مَا هُمْ ۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُودُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّاهُ فَاسْتَغْفَرَ رَبَّهُ وَخَرَّ رَاكِعًا وَأَنَابَ ۩

t.sahih-int: [David] said, "He has certainly wronged you in demanding your ewe [in addition] to his ewes. And indeed, many associates oppress one another, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds - and few are they." And David became certain that We had tried him, and he asked forgiveness of his Lord and fell down bowing [in prostration] and turned in repentance [to Allah]
t.asad: Said [David]: “He has certainly wronged thee by demanding that thy ewe be added to his ewes! Thus, behold, do many kinsmen wrong one another [all] save those who believe [in God] and do righteous deeds: but how few are they!” And [suddenly] David understood that We had tried him: and so he asked his Sustainer to forgive him his sin, and fell down in prostration, and turned unto Him in repentance
t.yusuf-ali: (David) said: "He has undoubtedly wronged thee in demanding thy (single) ewe to be added to his (flock of) ewes: truly many are the partners (in business) who wrong each other: Not so do those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, and how few are they?"... and David gathered that We had tried him: he asked forgiveness of his Lord, fell down, bowing (in prostration), and turned (to Allah in repentance)
t.pickthall: (David) said: He hath wronged thee in demanding thine ewe in addition to his ewes, and lo! many partners oppress one another, save such as believe and do good works, and they are few. And David guessed that We had tried him, and he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he bowed himself and fell down prostrate and repented
t.arberry: Said he, 'Assuredly he has wronged thee in asking for thy ewe in addition to his sheep; and indeed many intermixers do injury one against the other, save those who believe, and do deeds of righteousness -- and how few they are!' And David thought that We had only tried him; therefore he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down, bowing, and he repented
t.hilali-khan: Dawud (David)] said (immediately without listening to the opponent): "He has wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And, verily, many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and they are few." And Dawud (David) guessed that We have tried him and he sought Forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down prostrate and turned (to Allah) in repentance
t.itani: He said, “He has done you wrong by asking your ewe in addition to his ewes. Many partners take advantage of one another, except those who believe and do good deeds, but these are so few.” David realized that We were testing him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord, and fell down to his knees, and repented.
t.mubarakpuri: [Dawud] said: "He has wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And, verily, many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous good deeds, and they are few." And Dawud guessed that We have tried him and he sought forgiveness of his Lord, and he fell down prostrate and turned (to Allah) in repentance.
t.qarib: [David] said, "He has certainly wronged you in demanding that your ewe be added to his ewes. And many associates oppress one another, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds, and they are few." David then realized that We had been testing him, and he asked forgiveness of his Lord, and fell down bowing [in prostration], and turned [to God] in repentance
t.wahiduddin: David said, "He has certainly wronged you by demanding that your ewe be added to his ewes! Thus many partners wrong one another -- [all] save those who believe [in God] and do righteous deeds: but how few are they!" And [suddenly] David understood that We were only putting him to the test, and so he asked his Lord to forgive him his sins; he fell to his knees, and turned to Him in repentance
t.shakir: He said: Surely he has been unjust to you in demanding your ewe (to add) to his own ewes; and most surely most of the partners act wrongfully towards one another, save those who believe and do good, and very few are they; and Dawood was sure that We had tried him, so he sought the protection of his Lord and he fell down bowing and turned time after time (to Him)
t.haleem: David said, ‘He has done you wrong by demanding to add your ewe to his flock. Many partners treat each other unfairly. Those who sincerely believe and do good deeds do not do this, but these are very few.’ [Then] David realized that We had been testing him, so he asked his Lord for forgiveness, fell down on his knees, and repented
t.maariful: He (Dawūd) said, “He has certainly wronged you by demanding your ewe to be added to his ewe. Many partners oppress one another, except those who believe and do righteous deeds, and very few they are.” And Dawūd realized that We had put him to a test, so he prayed to his Lord for forgiveness, and bowing down, he fell in prostration, and turned (to Allah)
t.khattab: David ˹eventually˺ ruled, “He has definitely wronged you in demanding ˹to add˺ your sheep to his. And certainly many partners wrong each other, except those who believe and do good—but how few are they!” Then David realized that We had tested him so he asked for his Lord’s forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned ˹to Him in repentance˺
t.ghali: He (Dawud) said, "Indeed he has already done an injustice to you in asking for your ewe (in addition) to his ewes; and surely many intermixes are indeed inequitable one against the other, (Literally: some of them are unfair to some "others") except the ones who have believed and done deeds of righteousness, and how few they are!" And Dawud surmised that We had only tempted (Or: tried) him; so he asked forgiveness of his Lord, and he collapsed bowing down and turned penitent. A prostration is to be performed here
t.irving: He said: "He has wronged you by asking for your ewe [to be added] to his own ewes. Many partners try to take advantage of one another, except for those who believe and perform honorable deeds. Such are few indeed." David suspected that We were merely testing him, so he sought forgiveness from his Lord and dropped down on his knees [in worship], and repented
t.iwq: David said, "He has certainly wronged you in demanding your ewe in addition to his ewes. And indeed, many associates oppress one another, except for those who believe and do righteous deeds - and few are they." And David became certain that We had tried him, and he asked forgiveness of his Lord and fell down bowing in prostration and turned in repentance to Allah

t_id.kemenag: Daud berkata: "Sesungguhnya dia telah berbuat zalim kepadamu dengan meminta kambingmu itu untuk ditambahkan kepada kambingnya. Dan sesungguhnya kebanyakan dari orang-orang yang berserikat itu sebahagian mereka berbuat zalim kepada sebahagian yang lain, kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh; dan amat sedikitlah mereka ini". Dan Daud mengetahui bahwa Kami mengujinya; maka ia meminta ampun kepada Tuhannya lalu menyungkur sujud dan bertaubat.
t_id.sabiq: Sebelum mendengar pembicaraan yang seorang lagi, Dâwûd berkata, "Sesunguhnya ia telah menzalimi kamu dengan meminta menggabungkan kambingmu dengan kambing-kambingnya. Banyak, memang, di antara orang-orang berserikat yang saling merugikan satu sama lain. Kecuali orang-orang yang imannya merasuk dalam kalbunya dan selalu beramal saleh. Tetapi mereka yang seperti itu amat sedikit dan jarang." Dâwûd tahu bahwa ini hanya sekadar ujian dari Kami untuknya. Dia pun lalu meminta ampunan dari Allah, bersujud dan bertobat kepada-Nya dengan penuh kekhusyukan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Daud berkata, "Sesungguhnya dia telah berbuat lalim kepadamu dengan meminta kambingmu itu) dengan maksud untuk menggabungkannya (untuk ditambahkan kepada kambingnya. Sesungguhnya kebanyakan dari orang-orang yang berserikat itu) yakni orang-orang yang terlibat dalam satu perserikatan (sebagian mereka berbuat lalim kepada sebagian yang lain, kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh; dan amat sedikitlah mereka ini") huruf Ma di sini untuk mengukuhkan makna sedikit. Lalu kedua malaikat itu naik ke langit dalam keadaan berubah menjadi ujud aslinya seraya berkata, "Lelaki ini telah memutuskan perkara terhadap dirinya sendiri." Sehingga sadarlah Nabi Daud atas kekeliruannya itu. Lalu Allah berfirman, (Dan Daud yakin) yakni merasa yakin (bahwa Kami mengujinya) Kami menimpakan ujian kepadanya, berupa cobaan dalam bentuk cinta kepada perempuan itu (maka ia meminta ampun kepada Rabbnya lalu menyungkur rukuk) maksudnya bersujud (dan bertobat.)
t_id.iwq: Daud berkata: "Sesungguhnya dia telah berbuat zalim kepadamu dengan meminta kambingmu itu untuk ditambahkan kepada kambingnya. Dan sesungguhnya kebanyakan dari orang-orang yang berserikat itu sebahagian mereka berbuat zalim kepada sebahagian yang lain, kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh; dan amat sedikitlah mereka ini". Dan Daud mengetahui bahwa Kami mengujinya; maka ia meminta ampun kepada Tuhannya lalu menyungkur sujud dan bertaubat.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال لقد ظلمك بسؤال نعجتك» ليضمها «إلى نعاجه وإن كثيرا من الخلطاء» الشركاء «ليبغي بعضهم على بعض إلا الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات وقليل ما هم» ما لتأكيد القلة فقال الملكان صاعدين في صورتيهما إلى السماء: قضى الرجل على نفسه فتنبه داود قال تعالى: «وظن» أي أيقن «داود أنما فتناه» أوقعناه في فتنة أي بلية بمحبته تلك المرأة «فاستغفر ربَّه وخرَّ راكعا» أي ساجدا «وأناب»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ لَقَدۡ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعۡجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡخُلَطَآءِ لَيَبۡغِي بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٍ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَقَلِيلٞ مَّا هُمۡۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُۥدُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّـٰهُ فَٱسۡتَغۡفَرَ رَبَّهُۥ وَخَرَّۤ رَاكِعٗاۤ وَأَنَابَ۩ (الْخُلَطَاءِ: الشُّرَكَاءِ, لَيَبْغِي: لَيَعْتَدِي, وَظَنَّ: أَيْقَنَ, فَتَنَّاهُ: ابْتَلَيْنَاهُ، وَامْتَحَنَّاهُ, وَخَرَّ رَاكِعًا: سَجَدَ لِلهِ تَعَالَى, وَأَنَابَ: رَجَعَ، وَتَابَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Daud berkata, "Sesungguhnya dia telah berbuat lalim kepadamu dengan meminta kambingmu itu) dengan maksud untuk menggabungkannya (untuk ditambahkan kepada kambingnya. Sesungguhnya kebanyakan dari orang-orang yang berserikat itu) yakni orang-orang yang terlibat dalam satu perserikatan (sebagian mereka berbuat lalim kepada sebagian yang lain, kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh; dan amat sedikitlah mereka ini") huruf Ma di sini untuk mengukuhkan makna sedikit. Lalu kedua malaikat itu naik ke langit dalam keadaan berubah menjadi ujud aslinya seraya berkata, "Lelaki ini telah memutuskan perkara terhadap dirinya sendiri." Sehingga sadarlah Nabi Daud atas kekeliruannya itu. Lalu Allah berfirman, (Dan Daud yakin) yakni merasa yakin (bahwa Kami mengujinya) Kami menimpakan ujian kepadanya, berupa cobaan dalam bentuk cinta kepada perempuan itu (maka ia meminta ampun kepada Rabbnya lalu menyungkur rukuk) maksudnya bersujud (dan bertobat)

ar: فَغَفَرۡنَا لَهُۥ ذَ ٰلِكَۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَءَابࣲ
ar_simple: فَغَفَرْنَا لَهُ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُ عِنْدَنَا لَزُلْفَىٰ وَحُسْنَ مَآبٍ

t.sahih-int: So We forgave him that; and indeed, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return
t.asad: And thereupon We forgave him that [sin]: and, verily, nearness to Us awaits him [in the life to come], and the most beauteous of all goals
t.yusuf-ali: So We forgave him this (lapse): he enjoyed, indeed, a Near Approach to Us, and a beautiful place of (Final) Return
t.pickthall: So We forgave him that; and lo! he had access to Our presence and a happy journey's end
t.arberry: Accordingly We forgave him that, and he has a near place in Our presence and a fair resort
t.hilali-khan: So We forgave him that, and verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good place of (final) return (Paradise)
t.itani: So We forgave him that. And for him is nearness to Us, and a good place of return.
t.mubarakpuri: So, We forgave him that, and verily, for him is a near access to Us, and a good place of return.
t.qarib: so We forgave him, and, he has nearness to Us, and a good place in the Hereafter
t.wahiduddin: We forgave him his sins. His reward will be nearness to Us, a good place to return to
t.shakir: Therefore We rectified for him this, and most surely he had a nearness to Us and an excellent resort
t.haleem: We forgave him [his misdeed]. His reward will be nearness to Us, a good place to return to
t.maariful: So we forgave him that (lapse), and surely he has a place of nearness in Our presence, and an excellent resort
t.khattab: So We forgave that for him. And he will indeed have ˹a status of˺ closeness to Us and an honourable destination
t.ghali: So We forgave him that, and surely he has in Our Providence proximity indeed and a fair resorting
t.irving: So We forgave him that. He enjoys precedence with Us and the finest retreat
t.iwq: Thus We forgave him that; and verily, for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return

t_id.kemenag: Maka Kami ampuni baginya kesalahannya itu. Dan sesungguhnya dia mempunyai kedudukan dekat pada sisi Kami dan tempat kembali yang baik.
t_id.sabiq: Kami lalu mengampuni sikapnya yang tergesa-gesa dalam memberikan keputusan itu. Sesungguhnya di sisi Kami ia mempunyai kedudukan yang dekat dan tempat kembali yang baik.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Kami ampuni baginya kesalahannya itu. Dan sesungguhnya dia mempunyai kedudukan dekat pada sisi Kami) yakni dengan ditambahkan kebaikan baginya di dunia (dan tempat kembali yang baik) kelak di akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Maka Kami ampuni baginya kesalahannya itu. Dan sesungguhnya dia mempunyai kedudukan dekat pada sisi Kami dan tempat kembali yang baik.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فغفرنا له ذلك وإن له عندنا لزلفى» أي زيادة خير في الدنيا «وحسن مآب» مرجع في الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَغَفَرۡنَا لَهُۥ ذَٰلِكَۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ (لَزُلْفَى: لَقُرْبَى وَمَكَانَةً, مَآبٍ: مَرْجِعٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Kami ampuni baginya kesalahannya itu. Dan sesungguhnya dia mempunyai kedudukan dekat pada sisi Kami) yakni dengan ditambahkan kebaikan baginya di dunia (dan tempat kembali yang baik) kelak di akhirat

ar: یَـٰدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلۡنَـٰكَ خَلِیفَةࣰ فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱحۡكُم بَیۡنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلۡهَوَىٰ فَیُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِینَ یَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمۡ عَذَابࣱ شَدِیدُۢ بِمَا نَسُوا۟ یَوۡمَ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
ar_simple: يَا دَاوُودُ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَاكَ خَلِيفَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ فَاحْكُمْ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ الْهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ بِمَا نَسُوا يَوْمَ الْحِسَابِ

t.sahih-int: [We said], "O David, indeed We have made you a successor upon the earth, so judge between the people in truth and do not follow [your own] desire, as it will lead you astray from the way of Allah." Indeed, those who go astray from the way of Allah will have a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account
t.asad: [And We said:] “O David! Behold, We have made thee a [prophet and, thus, Our] vicegerent on earth: judge, then, between men with justice, and do not follow vain desire, lest it lead thee astray from the path of God: verily, for those who go astray from the path of God there is suffering severe in store for having forgotten the Day of Reckoning!”
t.yusuf-ali: O David! We did indeed make thee a vicegerent on earth: so judge thou between men in truth (and justice): Nor follow thou the lusts (of thy heart), for they will mislead thee from the Path of Allah: for those who wander astray from the Path of Allah, is a Penalty Grievous, for that they forget the Day of Account
t.pickthall: (And it was said unto him): O David! Lo! We have set thee as a viceroy in the earth; therefor judge aright between mankind, and follow not desire that it beguile thee from the way of Allah. Lo! those who wander from the way of Allah have an awful doom, forasmuch as they forgot the Day of Reckoning
t.arberry: David, behold, We have appointed thee a viceroy in the earth; therefore judge between men justly, and follow not caprice, lest it lead thee astray from the way of God. Surely those who go astray from the way of God -- there awaits them a terrible chastisement, for that they have forgotten the Day of Reckoning
t.hilali-khan: O Dawud (David)! Verily! We have placed you as a successor on earth, so judge you between men in truth (and justice) and follow not your desire for it will mislead you from the Path of Allah. Verily! Those who wander astray from the Path of Allah (shall) have a severe torment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning
t.itani: “O David, We made you a ruler in the land, so judge between the people with justice, and do not follow desire, lest it diverts you from God’s path. Those who stray from God’s path will have a painful punishment, for having ignored the Day of Account.”
t.mubarakpuri: O Dawud! Verily, We have placed you as a successor on the earth; so judge you between men in truth (and justice) and follow not your desire -- for it will mislead you from the path of Allah. Verily, those who wander astray from the path of Allah (shall) have a severe torment, because they forgot the Day of Reckoning.
t.qarib: [We said], "David, We have made you a steward over this land, so judge between the people in truth and do not follow [your own] whim, as it will lead you away from the way of God." Those who wander away from His path will have a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account
t.wahiduddin: We said, "David, We have given you mastery over the land. Judge fairly between people. Do not follow your desires, lest they divert you from God's path: those who wander from His path will have a severe punishment, because they ignore the Day of Reckoning
t.shakir: o Dawood! surely We have made you a ruler in the land; so judge between men with justice and do not follow desire, lest it should lead you astray from the path of Allah; (as for) those who go astray from the path of Allah, they shall surely have a severe punishment because they forgot the day of reckoning
t.haleem: ‘David, We have given you mastery over the land. Judge fairly between people. Do not follow your desires, lest they divert you from God’s path: those who wander from His path will have a painful torment because they ignore the Day of Reckoning.’
t.maariful: O Dawūd, We have made you a vicegerent on earth, so judge between people with truth, and do not follow the selfish desire, lest it should lead you astray from Allah’s path. Surely those who go astray from Allah’s path will have a severe punishment, because they had forgotten the Day of Reckoning
t.khattab: ˹We instructed him:˺ “O David! We have surely made you an authority in the land, so judge between people with truth. And do not follow ˹your˺ desires or they will lead you astray from Allah’s Way. Surely those who go astray from Allah’s Way will suffer a severe punishment for neglecting the Day of Reckoning.”
t.ghali: O Dawud, surely We have made you a succeeding (Literally: Caliph) (Messenger) in the earth, so judge among mankind with the truth, and do not ever follow prejudice that it may make you err from the way of Allah. Surely the ones who err from the way of Allah will have a strict torment for that they have forgotten the Day of Reckoning
t.irving: David, We have placed you as an overlord on earth, so judge among men correctly and do not follow any whims which will lead you away from God´s path. Those who stray from God´s path will have severe torment because they have forgotten the Day of Reckoning
t.iwq: We said, "O David, indeed We have made you a successor upon the earth, so judge between the people in truth and do not follow your own desire, as it will lead you astray from the way of Allah." Indeed, those who go astray from the way of Allah will have a severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Account

t_id.kemenag: Hai Daud, sesungguhnya Kami menjadikan kamu khalifah (penguasa) di muka bumi, maka berilah keputusan (perkara) di antara manusia dengan adil dan janganlah kamu mengikuti hawa nafsu, karena ia akan menyesatkan kamu dari jalan Allah. Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang sesat darin jalan Allah akan mendapat azab yang berat, karena mereka melupakan hari perhitungan.
t_id.sabiq: Allah memberikan wahyu kepadanya dengan berfirman, "Hai Dâwûd, sesungguhnya Kami telah menjadikan kamu sebagai khalifah Kami di muka bumi. Oleh karena itu, berilah keputusan di antara manusia sesuai dengan syariat Kami. Jangan mengikuti hawa nafsu dalam mengambil keputusan sehingga kamu keluar dari jalan Allah. Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang keluar dari jalan Allah dengan mengikuti hawa nafsu akan mendapat azab yang pedih, karena lalai akan hari pembalasan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Hai Daud, sesungguhnya Kami menjadikan kamu khalifah penguasa di muka bumi) yaitu sebagai penguasa yang mengatur perkara manusia (maka berilah keputusan perkara di antara manusia dengan adil dan janganlah kamu mengikuti hawa nafsu) kemauan hawa nafsu (karena ia akan menyesatkan kamu dari jalan Allah) dari bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan keesaan-Nya. (Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang sesat dari jalan Allah) dari iman kepada Allah (mereka akan mendapat siksa yang berat karena mereka melupakan) artinya, disebabkan mereka lupa akan (hari perhitungan) hal ini ditunjukkan oleh sikap mereka yang tidak mau beriman, seandainya mereka beriman dengan adanya hari perhitungan itu, niscaya mereka akan beriman kepada Allah sewaktu mereka di dunia.
t_id.iwq: Hai Daud, sesungguhnya Kami menjadikan kamu khalifah (penguasa) di muka bumi, maka berilah keputusan (perkara) di antara manusia dengan adil dan janganlah kamu mengikuti hawa nafsu, karena ia akan menyesatkan kamu dari jalan Allah. Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang sesat darin jalan Allah akan mendapat azab yang berat, karena mereka melupakan hari perhitungan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا داود إنا جعلناك خليفة في الأرض» تدبر أمر الناس «فاحكم بين الناس بالحق ولا تتبع الهوى» أي هوى النفس «فيضلك عن سبيل الله» أي عن الدلائل الدالة على توحيده «إن الذين يضلون عن سبيل الله» أي عن الإيمان بالله «لهم عذاب شديد بما نسوا» بنسيانهم «يوم الحساب» المرتب عليه تركهم الإيمان، وقالوا أيقنوا بيوم الحساب لآمنوا في الدنيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَٰدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلۡنَٰكَ خَلِيفَةٗ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلۡهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمۡ عَذَابٞ شَدِيدُۢ بِمَا نَسُواْ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Hai Daud, sesungguhnya Kami menjadikan kamu khalifah penguasa di muka bumi) yaitu sebagai penguasa yang mengatur perkara manusia (maka berilah keputusan perkara di antara manusia dengan adil dan janganlah kamu mengikuti hawa nafsu) kemauan hawa nafsu (karena ia akan menyesatkan kamu dari jalan Allah) dari bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan keesaan-Nya. (Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang sesat dari jalan Allah) dari iman kepada Allah (mereka akan mendapat siksa yang berat karena mereka melupakan) artinya, disebabkan mereka lupa akan (hari perhitungan) hal ini ditunjukkan oleh sikap mereka yang tidak mau beriman, seandainya mereka beriman dengan adanya hari perhitungan itu, niscaya mereka akan beriman kepada Allah sewaktu mereka di dunia

ar: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَاۤءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَیۡنَهُمَا بَـٰطِلࣰاۚ ذَ ٰلِكَ ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ۚ فَوَیۡلࣱ لِّلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ
ar_simple: وَمَا خَلَقْنَا السَّمَاءَ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا بَاطِلًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنَ النَّارِ

t.sahih-int: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and that between them aimlessly. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve, so woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire
t.asad: AND [thus it is:] We have not created heaven and earth and all that is between them without meaning and purpose, as is the surmise of those who are bent on denying the truth: but then, woe from the fire [of hell] unto all who are bent on denying the truth
t.yusuf-ali: Not without purpose did We create heaven and earth and all between! that were the thought of Unbelievers! but woe to the Unbelievers because of the Fire (of Hell)
t.pickthall: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them in vain. That is the opinion of those who disbelieve. And woe unto those who disbelieve, from the Fire
t.arberry: We have not created the heavens and earth, and what between them is, for vanity; such is the thought of the unbelievers, wherefore woe unto the unbelievers because of the Fire
t.hilali-khan: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose! That is the consideration of those who disbelieve! Then woe to those who disbelieve (in Islamic Monotheism) from the Fire
t.itani: We did not create the heaven and the earth and everything between them in vain. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve—so woe to those who disbelieve because of the Fire.
t.mubarakpuri: And We created not the heaven and the earth and all that is between them without purpose! That is the consideration of those who disbelieve! Then woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire!
t.qarib: We have not created the heaven and the earth and all that lies between them without meaning and purpose. That is the assumption of the unbelievers. How terrible the fire will be for the unbelievers
t.wahiduddin: We did not create heaven and earth and all that is between them in vain. That is the opinion of those who deny the truth. Woe betide those who deny the truth, when they are cast into the Fire
t.shakir: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and what is between them in vain; that is the opinion of those who disbelieve then woe to those who disbelieve on account of the fire
t.haleem: It was not without purpose that We created the heavens and the earth and everything in between. That may be what the disbelievers assume- how they will suffer from the Fire
t.maariful: We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them in vain. That is the thinking of those who disbelieve. So, woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire (they have to face)
t.khattab: We have not created the heavens and earth and everything in between without purpose—as the disbelievers think. So woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire
t.ghali: And in no way have We created the heaven and the earth and whatever is between them untruthfully. That is the surmise of the ones who have disbelieved. So woe to the ones who have disbelieved, from the Fire
t.irving: We did not create Heaven and Earth and whatever [lies] in between them to no purpose. That is the opinion of those who disbelieve. Those who disbelieve should beware of the Fire
t.iwq: And We did not create the heaven and the earth and that between them aimlessly. That is the assumption of those who disbelieve, so woe to those who disbelieve from the Fire

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami tidak menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada antara keduanya tanpa hikmah. Yang demikian itu adalah anggapan orang-orang kafir, maka celakalah orang-orang kafir itu karena mereka akan masuk neraka.
t_id.sabiq: Kami tidak menciptakan langit dan bumi beserta semua yang ada di antara keduanya dengan sia-sia. Itu hanya sangkaan orang-orang kafir sehingga mereka semena-mena memberikan keputusan sesuai hawa nafsunya. Dari itu, mereka akan memperoleh siksa yang pedih berupa api neraka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami tidak menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya dengan batil) dengan main-main. (Yang demikian itu) yakni penciptaan hal tersebut tanpa hikmah (adalah anggapan orang-orang kafir) dari penduduk Mekah (maka neraka Waillah) Wail adalah nama sebuah lembah di neraka (bagi orang-orang yang kafir karena mereka akan masuk neraka.)
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami tidak menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada antara keduanya tanpa hikmah. Yang demikian itu adalah anggapan orang-orang kafir, maka celakalah orang-orang kafir itu karena mereka akan masuk neraka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما خلقنا السماء والأرض وما بينهما باطلا» عبثا «ذلك» أي خلق ما ذكر لا لشيء «ظن الذين كفروا» من أهل مكة «فويل» وادِ «للذين كفروا من النار»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا بَٰطِلٗاۚ ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْۚ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ (بَاطِلًا: عَبَثًا وَلَهْوًا, فَوَيْلٌ: فَهَلَاكٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami tidak menciptakan langit dan bumi dan apa yang ada di antara keduanya dengan batil) dengan main-main. (Yang demikian itu) yakni penciptaan hal tersebut tanpa hikmah (adalah anggapan orang-orang kafir) dari penduduk Mekah (maka neraka Waillah) Wail adalah nama sebuah lembah di neraka (bagi orang-orang yang kafir karena mereka akan masuk neraka)

ar: أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ كَٱلۡمُفۡسِدِینَ فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلۡمُتَّقِینَ كَٱلۡفُجَّارِ
ar_simple: أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ كَالْمُفْسِدِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَمْ نَجْعَلُ الْمُتَّقِينَ كَالْفُجَّارِ

t.sahih-int: Or should we treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like corrupters in the land? Or should We treat those who fear Allah like the wicked
t.asad: [For,] would We treat those who have attained to faith and do righteous deeds in the same manner as [We shall treat] those who spread corruption on earth? Would We treat the God-conscious in the same manner as the wicked
t.yusuf-ali: Shall We treat those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, the same as those who do mischief on earth? Shall We treat those who guard against evil, the same as those who turn aside from the right
t.pickthall: Shall We treat those who believe and do good works as those who spread corruption in the earth; or shall We treat the pious as the wicked
t.arberry: Or shall We make those who believe and do righteous deeds as the workers of corruption in the earth, or shall We make the godfearing as the transgressors
t.hilali-khan: Shall We treat those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, as Mufsidun (those who associate partners in worship with Allah and commit crimes) on earth? Or shall We treat the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2), as the Fujjar (criminals, disbelievers, wicked, etc)
t.itani: Or are We to treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like those who make trouble on earth? Or are We to treat the pious like the shameless?
t.mubarakpuri: Shall We treat those who believe and do righteous good deeds as those who cause mischief on the earth Or shall We treat those who have Taqwa as the evildoers
t.qarib: Would we treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like those who spread corruption on earth? Or would We treat those who are mindful of God like the wicked
t.wahiduddin: shall We treat those who believe and do good works the same as those who spread corruption in the land; shall We treat the pious the same as the wicked
t.shakir: Shall We treat those who believe and do good like the mischief-makers in the earth? Or shall We make those who guard (against evil) like the wicked
t.haleem: but would We treat those who believe and do good deeds and those who spread corruption on earth as equal? Would We treat those who are aware of God and those who recklessly break all bounds in the same way
t.maariful: Shall We make those who believe and do righteous deeds equal to those who commit mischief on the earth? Or shall We make the God-fearing equal to the sinners
t.khattab: Or should We treat those who believe and do good like those who make mischief throughout the land? Or should We treat the righteous like the wicked
t.ghali: Or even shall We make the ones who have believed and done deeds of righteousness as the corruptors in the earth, or even shall We make the pious as the constantly impious
t.irving: Shall We treat those who believe and perform honorable deeds just like mischief-makers on earth? Or should We treat those who do their duty, like lawbreakers
t.iwq: Or should we treat those who believe and do good deeds like corrupters in the land? Or should We treat those who fear Allah like the wicked

t_id.kemenag: Patutkah Kami menganggap orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat kerusakan di muka bumi? Patutkah (pula) Kami menganggap orang-orang yang bertakwa sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat maksiat?
t_id.sabiq: Apakah sesuai dengan hikmah (kebijakan) dan keadilan Kami, menganggap sama antara orang-orang Mukmin yang berbuat baik dan orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan di muka bumi. Dan apakah patut bagi Kami menganggap sama antara orang-orang yang takut akan siksa Kami dan orang-orang yang keluar dari ketentuan hukum Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Patutkah Kami menganggap orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat kerusakan di muka bumi? Patutkah pula Kami menganggap orang-orang yang bertakwa sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat maksiat?) Ayat ini diturunkan sewaktu orang-orang kafir Mekah berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman, "Sesungguhnya kami kelak di hari kemudian akan diberi seperti apa yang diberikan kepada kalian." Lafal Am di sini untuk menunjukkan makna sanggahan, yakni jelas tidak sama.
t_id.iwq: Patutkah Kami menganggap orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat kerusakan di muka bumi? Patutkah (pula) Kami menganggap orang-orang yang bertakwa sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat maksiat?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم نجعل الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات كالمفسدين في الأرض أم نجعل المتقين كالفجار» نزل لما قال كفار مكة للمؤمنين إنا نعطى في الآخرة مثل ما تعطون، وأم بمعنى همزة الإنكار
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ كَٱلۡمُفۡسِدِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ كَٱلۡفُجَّارِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Patutkah Kami menganggap orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan amal yang saleh sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat kerusakan di muka bumi? Patutkah pula Kami menganggap orang-orang yang bertakwa sama dengan orang-orang yang berbuat maksiat?) Ayat ini diturunkan sewaktu orang-orang kafir Mekah berkata kepada orang-orang yang beriman, "Sesungguhnya kami kelak di hari kemudian akan diberi seperti apa yang diberikan kepada kalian." Lafal Am di sini untuk menunjukkan makna sanggahan, yakni jelas tidak sama

ar: كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلۡنَـٰهُ إِلَیۡكَ مُبَـٰرَكࣱ لِّیَدَّبَّرُوۤا۟ ءَایَـٰتِهِۦ وَلِیَتَذَكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلۡأَلۡبَـٰبِ
ar_simple: كِتَابٌ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ إِلَيْكَ مُبَارَكٌ لِيَدَّبَّرُوا آيَاتِهِ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ

t.sahih-int: [This is] a blessed Book which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], that they might reflect upon its verses and that those of understanding would be reminded
